schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill

identity.canonical_title: TZP Tunnel to ER Bridge Coupled Mode

theory.canonical_statement: The TZP tunnel to ER bridge source gives both an ER coupling Hamiltonian and a coupled-mode evolution equation for the alpha mode.
theory.technical_interpretation: The equation support layer follows H_{\mathrm{ER}}=\sum_k\lambda_k(a^\dagger+a)E_k+\sum_k\mu_k(b^\dagger+b)E_k; \dot{\alpha}=-i\omega_a\alpha-\gamma_a\alpha-iJ\beta-iK|\alpha|^2\alpha-i g_a\gamma; \Gamma_{\mathrm{tunnel}}\propto\exp(-S_{\mathrm{bridge}}/\hbar). These relations are compact review objects, not unsupported placeholders.

reviewed_source_symbols.description: natural angular frequency of the alpha mode.
loss rate for the alpha mode.

dictionary_sections.definition: TZP Tunnel to ER Bridge Coupled Mode is a source-backed registry entry anchored by \dot{\alpha}=-i\omega_a\alpha-\gamma_a\alpha-iJ\beta-iK|\alpha|^2\alpha-i g_a\gamma.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The TZP tunnel to ER bridge source gives both an ER coupling Hamiltonian and a coupled-mode evolution equation for the alpha mode. The packet keeps the entry usable in the folder-local production layout while preserving review boundaries.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The repaired relation supplies a canonical equation, a source-specific symbol key, a Wolfram model, and a derivation chain for production preview.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Source extraction; equation normalization; symbol-key grounding; TRAWIN closure compatibility.

These relations are compact review objects, not unsupported placeholders.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Grounded source: `D:/00_Engine/0_Incoming/Gemini_Exports/Takeout/Drive/tzptunnel_to_erbridge.docx`. The canonical equation is \dot{\alpha}=-i\omega_a\alpha-\gamma_a\alpha-iJ\beta-iK|\alpha|^2\alpha-i g_a\gamma.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This packet converts malformed or empty registry material into a usable production section based on cited D-drive evidence.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the repaired mathematical relation used as the entry spine.
\item Source grounding: the D-drive file or row that supplies the relation.
\item TRAWIN closure: the interpretation through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: A suitable visual should place the canonical relation at center, source operators as labeled inputs, and the equation-support progression as the lower derivation path.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
H_{\mathrm{ER}}=\sum_k\lambda_k(a^\dagger+a)E_k+\sum_k\mu_k(b^\dagger+b)E_k
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\dot{\alpha}=-i\omega_a\alpha-\gamma_a\alpha-iJ\beta-iK|\alpha|^2\alpha-i g_a\gamma
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\Gamma_{\mathrm{tunnel}}\propto\exp(-S_{\mathrm{bridge}}/\hbar)
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
